body.probe: Igor restarts cleanly with no thread_anchor warning in logs; thread anchor appears in session context on first turn
body.title: thread_anchor read fails: _PGConnWrapper has no attribute 'cursor'
body.result: One-line fix: read_thread_anchor() called conn.cursor() on _PGConnWrapper which doesn't expose .cursor(). Changed to conn.execute() + conn.fetchall() directly. Thread anchors now inject correctly into session context on every boot.
body.description: After restart, thread_anchor read fails with: '_PGConnWrapper' object has no attribute 'cursor'. The read_thread_anchor() function is calling .cursor() directly on the wrapper instead of using it as a context manager (with conn() as c: c.cursor()). Affects thread continuity across restarts — Igor loses the thread anchor injection into _build_session_context on every cold start.
